nav.invoice.desc: Upload Excel/CSV · IFRS/VAS posting · into cash flow
inv.title: Read invoices from file
inv.subtitle: Upload invoice listing (Excel/CSV) → VAS/IFRS posting → into cash flow
inv.err.noHeader: Could not recognise the column headers. The file needs a header row with an invoice number and a total — e.g. Invoice No. / Total (or Số HĐ / Tổng thanh toán). Exports from QuickBooks, Xero, MISA and FAST are read as-is.
inv.err.noRows: Column headers were read, but the file has no invoice rows below them.
inv.empty.title: Upload an invoice listing
inv.empty.desc: Supports .xlsx / .xls / .csv exported from MISA meInvoice, FAST, QuickBooks or Xero. The system auto-detects the header row, maps columns (Invoice No. · Tax ID · Net · Tax rate · Total…), posts entries per accounting standard and feeds receivables into the cash-flow forecast.
inv.stage.chk.d: Check totals, Tax ID, tax rate
inv.detail.title: Detail & posting · Inv {no}
inv.cash.title: 13-week cash flow — before & after collecting these invoices
inv.cash.spread: Total receivable +{v} is spread across weeks by invoice date + ~4-week terms · unit: million.
inv.cash.running: Running analysis to merge receivables into cash flow…
inv.footer: Reads your uploaded Excel/CSV directly · classification per VAS (Circular 200) / IFRS · not accounting advice — have an accountant review before posting.
cf.title: Cash Flow AI
cf.mode.empty.tip: No cash flow data for this company yet — click 'Enter data' to start
cf.empty.title: No cash flow data yet
cf.empty.desc: Enter opening cash, receivables and payables so the forecast runs on your company's real numbers. Credit-sale invoices uploaded in Invoice Reader also create receivables automatically.
cf.classDesc.real: Aggregated automatically from uploaded invoices and your receivables/payables ledger.
cf.class.empty: Nothing to classify yet — upload invoices in Invoice Reader or add receivables/payables.
cf.data.bar.title: Cash flow data
cf.data.bar.desc: Update opening cash, receivables and payables.
cf.mode.real.tip: Forecast runs on your company's data
cf.mode.demo.tip: Sample data — click 'Enter data' to enter your company's numbers
cf.data.title: Company cash flow data
cf.data.subtitle: Enter opening cash, receivables (customer debts) and payables (payroll, rent, suppliers…). With at least 1 row, the forecast switches to live data.
cf.data.recv.empty: No receivables yet — add below, or upload credit-sale invoices in Invoice Reader.
cf.data.pay.empty: No payables yet — add payroll, office rent, supplier bills…
cf.data.label: Description (payroll, rent…)
cf.data.fromInvoice: Auto-created from an invoice uploaded in Invoice Reader
cf.data.err.fill: Fill in name, amount and due date
cf.data.note: Note: forecast inflow = collection of receivables; rows marked "Paid" are excluded. Credit-sale invoices uploaded in Invoice Reader appear here automatically (due +30 days).
cf.recv.empty: No receivables yet — click "Real data" to enter them, or upload credit-sale invoices in Invoice Reader.
cf.subtitle: Forecast · Stress-test · Early warning · Collection plan
mod.partOf.prefix: This module is part of the
mod.partOf.suffix: suite. The full interactive version is available separately — this page shows a capability summary within the unified software.
cf.stressDesc: — simulate cash flow under market volatility
cf.earlyWarn: Early warning: {p}% chance of cash deficit within 13 weeks — {lvl}
cf.safeMsg: 1,400 simulations: expected trough {min}. The current plan withstands the "{sc}" scenario.
cf.chartUnit: Unit: million · shaded band is the P10–P90 range
cf.detectSub: chance the cash balance turns negative
cf.opt.title: AI-suggested optimal collection plan
cf.opt.desc1: AI scans every customer combination to find the
cf.opt.desc3: to push deficit risk below your chosen threshold.
cf.opt.applied: (meets ≤ {t}% threshold). Applied to the list below.
cf.opt.fail: Even collecting all 6 customers, risk remains
cf.opt.failTail: — above the ≤ {t}% threshold. Consider deferring payments to suppliers, negotiating credit limits, or arranging working capital.
cf.list.title: Recovery-probability model & collection call list
cf.list.desc: Probability scored by: days overdue · on-time payment history · debt size.
cf.list.scenario: The "{sc}" scenario lowers recovery probability by {p}%.
cf.classDesc: Matches entry content to the VAS (Circular 200) / IFRS chart of accounts
cf.footer: Demo · Monte Carlo 1,400 scenarios · Benchmarked vs Anaplan / Vena / Clockwork AI · Not financial advice — have an accountant review before deciding.
rp.tip: Tip: in the print dialog, choose
rp.title: CASH FLOW & RISK ALERT REPORT
rp.chartNote: Unit: million · shaded area is the 80% confidence band (P10–P90) · solid line is expected (P50)
rp.sec3: Receivables to collect · {n} items in plan
rp.disclaimer: Report auto-generated by LUXORA from the invoices you uploaded and your receivables/payables ledger, posted under the accounting standard on your company profile. Figures are forecasts, not financial advice — have an accountant review before deciding.
rp.verdict.safe: Under the "{sc}" scenario, cash is forecast safe across all 13 weeks (deficit probability {p}%). Expected cash trough reaches {min}. We recommend keeping the current collection plan and continuing weekly monitoring.
rp.verdict.risk: Under the "{sc}" scenario, cash has a {p}% chance of turning negative within 13 weeks (risk level: {lvl}), concentrated in Week {w} ({range}). Expected trough {min}, worst case {worst}. We recommend prioritizing collection of {n} key receivables (expected value {ev}) ahead of the heavy-spending weeks.
fpa.subtitle: A financial decision system — replacing manual Excel
fpa.intro1: Three automation layers replacing the Excel workflow:
fpa.p1.title: Smart cash flow forecast
fpa.p1.desc: LSTM time series combined with macro signals · the shaded band is the confidence interval (uncertainty grows over the horizon).
fpa.p2.title: Macro signal inputs
fpa.p2.desc: The model auto-updates external variables and measures their impact on the forecast.
fpa.signalText: raw material prices +6.5% is the strongest driver — the model auto-triggers the Stress-test scenario.
fpa.p3.title: Automated risk detection
fpa.p3.desc1: Auto-scans overdue receivables, abnormal costs & liquidity shortfall risk — alerting
fpa.footer: Demo of the FP&A Automation pillar · simulated data & connections · LSTM model + macro variables · integrates with the operations agent & auto collections.
fpa.al.a1.d: Minh Phat Co. — 320M overdue 45 days, exceeding credit limit. Risk of becoming bad debt.
fpa.al.a2.d: The model forecasts cash hitting a trough below the safety threshold in Week 6 due to clustered payroll + tax + supplier payments.
fpa.al.a3.d: Logistics cost this month +38% vs the 6-month average — abnormally high, review shipping contracts.
fpa.al.a4.d: 4 customers (215M total) will become overdue within 7–14 days. Proactively send reminders.
ops.title: Operations Agent ↔ Cash Flow
ops.subtitle1: Every purchase order self-checks: enough stock
ops.chart.title: 13-week cash flow forecast
ops.chart.unit: Unit: million · red dashed line = impact if the full order is placed now.
ops.inv.title: Inventory monitor
ops.idle2: to simulate sales. When a SKU hits its threshold, the agent drafts an order then
ops.conflict: Conflict: ordering in full runs short on cash!
ops.safeText: Ordering in full still keeps the cash trough at {min} (above the {floor} safety floor).
ops.conflictText1: Ordering in full now drags the cash trough down to
ops.conflictText2: . The agent suggests a balancing option:
ops.footer: Demo · the operations agent syncs directly with the cash-flow model · safety floor {floor} · not financial advice.
ops.step.cash: Feed PO into the 13-week cash forecast
ops.strat.full: Order in full, pay on terms
ops.strat.splitNote: Order the remaining {rest} units in 3 weeks
ops.log.boot: Agent started · monitoring inventory & syncing cash flow in real time.
ops.log.s2safe: Feed PO into forecast → cash trough stays safe.
ops.log.s2risk: Feed PO into forecast → cash trough drops to {min}{neg}.
ops.log.s3safe: Enough stock & enough cash → keep the full order.
ops.log.s3conflict: Stock ↔ cash conflict! Chose "{label}" → lifts the cash trough to {min}.
ops.log.approve: ✓ Approved {id} ({label}). Pay {cost} in Week {payW}, goods arrive Week {arrW}.
col.title: Auto Collections
col.subtitle: AI drafts & schedules collection reminders by urgency · multi-channel
col.list.title: Collection list
col.list.footer: Bar = recovery probability · priority: most overdue & highest value first.
col.subject: Subject:
col.sched.title: Reminder & escalation schedule
col.footer: Demo · AI drafts collection messages by urgency · multi-channel Email/Zalo/SMS · auto-schedule & escalation · not legal advice.
cr.title: Credit Scoring Agent
cr.subtitle: Score B2B partners & recommend safe credit limits
cr.datasources: Data sources: financial reports · payment history · industry data — auto-aggregated & scored.
cr.idle.hint: Click Score to let AI analyze the partner's financial reports, payment history, and industry data.
cr.limit.title: Recommended safe credit limit
cr.limit.good: Strong profile (grade {g}) — can approve the full {req} limit requested, with periodic monitoring.
cr.limit.reduced: At grade {g} ({label}), AI recommends {safe} instead of {req} to control bad-debt risk. Weakest factor: {weak}.
cr.footer: Demo · transparent weighted scoring model · simulated data · integrates with the operations agent & cash-flow forecast · not credit advice.

pr.agent.ware.desc: Real-time inventory monitoring, auto-draft purchase orders & optimize delivery routes.

pr.agent.ware.f5: Score & pick the best supplier (price · lead time · reliability)

pr.agent.credit.f3: Early alert for customers at risk of bad debt
pr.agent.logi.name: Logistics Optimization Agent
pr.agent.logi.desc: Consolidate shipments, pick the cheapest route & carrier in real time.
pr.agent.logi.f2: Consolidate orders to cut freight costs
pr.agent.collect.name: Auto Collection Agent

pr.agent.collect.f1: Auto-draft reminders tailored to each debtor

onb.title: Create your company profile
onb.subtitle: Pick a country to auto-set the default language, currency, accounting standard, tax regime & timezone — editable anytime.
onb.name: Company name
onb.standard.hint: Statutory is what you file with the local authority. Reporting is what you send your parent company or investors. An FDI company in Vietnam usually files under VAS and reports under IFRS.
inv.stdView.hint: Your statutory and reporting standards differ — switch to see the entries under each.
onb.err.name: Please enter a company name
settings.title: Company profile
settings.subtitle: Language, currency, accounting standard & timezone applied across the whole app.
settings.locked.hint: Regional settings (country, language, currency, accounting standard, timezone) are locked after creation — only the company name can be changed. Need a different setup? Create a new profile below.
settings.newProfile.title: New company profile
settings.newProfile.subtitle: The new profile becomes your active profile. The old one is kept.
settings.switchHint: Click a profile to switch to it — invoice history, currency and accounting standard follow the profile.
